name: Crostini Di Polenta
recipeInstructions:
1. For the polenta: In a 4-quart saucepan coated with cooking spray, sauté onion over low heat until tender, about 5 minutes.
2. Stir in broth. In a separate bowl, combine water and cornmeal; stir into the broth and cook, stirring until the mixture boils and thickens, about 10 minutes.
3. Add cheese and salt to taste.
4. Line a 9x9-inch baking pan with foil and spray with cooking spray. Spread polenta in a smooth layer and refrigerate until cold and firm, about 2 hours.
5. Turn polenta out of the pan and peel off the foil. Cut into triangles.
6. Preheat oven to 425°F. Spray a non-stick baking sheet with cooking spray.
7. Arrange triangles on the pan without touching. Bake on the bottom rack until browned on the bottom, about 15 minutes.
8. Turn and bake until browned and crisp.
9. Meanwhile, cut peppers into even portions. Sprinkle with herbs. Arrange on top of each triangle.
10. Serve warm or at room temperature.
